model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Saint Mary's holds a clear statistical edge as the 7-seed, ranking 24th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +23.11 compared to Texas A&M's +18.65 at 39th, driven primarily by an elite defense ranked 22nd nationally (AdjD 97.2) that is nearly 4 points per 100 possessions better than Texas A&M's 101.1. While Texas A&M's SEC schedule provides legitimate battle-testing with quality wins at Auburn and Pitt, their 21-11 record with 11 losses reflects significant inconsistency, and their offense (AdjO 119.7) offers no advantage over Saint Mary's comparable attack (AdjO 120.3). The 7/10 seed line is historically upset-prone and Texas A&M's power-conference physicality gives them a credible path, but Saint Mary's superior defensive identity and 4.5-point efficiency margin advantage make the Gaels the deserving favorite.

reasoningStep.title: South 7/10: Saint Mary's vs Texas A&M
reasoningStep.summary: Saint Mary's is the stronger team by a meaningful margin in both KenPom (#24 vs #39) and Torvik (#25 vs #39), with the gap driven by an elite defense. The Gaels' 27-5 record and defensive consistency give them the edge over an inconsistent 21-11 Texas A&M squad, though the SEC's schedule strength and the historically volatile 7/10 seed line temper confidence.
evidence: Saint Mary's KenPom #24 (AdjEM +23.11, AdjO 120.3, AdjD 97.2) vs Texas A&M KenPom #39 (AdjEM +18.65, AdjO 119.7, AdjD 101.1)
Saint Mary's Torvik #25 (AdjDE 22nd) vs Texas A&M Torvik #39 (AdjDE 43rd) — nearly 4-point defensive efficiency gap
Saint Mary's 27-5 with wins over Gonzaga (twice), Vanderbilt, Virginia Tech; Texas A&M 21-11 with 11 losses including multiple blowouts
Offenses are essentially equal (~119.7-120.3 AdjO), making Saint Mary's defensive superiority the decisive factor
7/10 seed line historically produces upsets ~40% of the time, but Saint Mary's is the higher seed and clearly better team by metrics

modelTrace.content:
Now I have a good picture. Let me analyze the data:

**Saint Mary's (7-seed)**
- Record: 27-5, 1st in WCC
- KenPom: #24, AdjO 120.3 (43rd), AdjD 97.2, AdjEM +23.11
- Torvik: #25, AdjO 119.4 (48th), AdjD 97.6 (22nd)
- Notable wins: beat Gonzaga twice (including 70-59 in WCC tournament), beat Vanderbilt, Virginia Tech, Wichita State
- Losses: Vanderbilt (96-71), Boise St (68-67), Oregon St (62-54), Gonzaga (73-65), Santa Clara (76-71 in what appears to be WCC tournament final)
- Elite defense (22nd Torvik, top-tier KenPom)

**Texas A&M (10-seed)**
- Record: 21-11, 4th in SEC
- KenPom: #39, AdjO 119.7 (49th), AdjD 101.1, AdjEM +18.65
- Torvik: #39, AdjO 119.9 (41st), AdjD 101.3 (43rd)
- Notable wins: Auburn, Pitt, LSU (multiple), Kentucky
- Losses: Oklahoma St, UCF, SMU, Tennessee, Alabama, Florida, Missouri, Vanderbilt, Arkansas, Texas (twice), Oklahoma (SEC tournament)
- 11 losses is a lot; inconsistent season

**Key Analysis:**
- Saint Mary's has a ~4.5-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (23.11 vs 18.65)
- The gap is primarily on defense: Saint Mary's AdjD 97.2 vs Texas A&M's 101.1 — a nearly 4-point difference
- Offenses are essentially equal (120.3 vs 119.7)
- Saint Mary's 27-5 record is much cleaner than Texas A&M's 21-11
- The WCC schedule is weaker than the SEC, but Saint Mary's has quality non-conference wins (Vanderbilt, Virginia Tech)
- Texas A&M has SEC battle-testing with wins at Auburn and at Pitt, but also many losses
- The 7/10 line historically produces upsets ~40% of the time, but Saint Mary's is the higher seed and the better team by metrics
- Saint Mary's lost the WCC tournament final to Santa Clara 76-71, so they enter without conference tournament championship momentum

This is a 7/10 matchup where the 7-seed is clearly the better team by metrics. Saint Mary's defensive excellence should be the differentiator.
